text.title: Agent Patterns Core Rules
text.summary: Six testable rules every agent-pattern decision enforces. Hard rules — operator input cannot relax them.

rule.statement: Every pattern carries a hard max_iterations (ReAct) or max_attempts (Reflexion) or max_steps (Plan-and-Execute). No unbounded loops in production.

rationale.source: ReAct paper, Yao et al. 2022 (arXiv:2210.03629); production agent harnesses

rule.rationale: Unbounded loops are the #1 cost-runaway and the most common production incident. Bound is a hard pre-condition for any pattern.

rule.statement: All tools the agent calls must be idempotent at the level of the underlying side effect, OR the call must be guarded by a deduplication key derived from the tool arguments.

rationale.source: distributed-systems: at-least-once delivery; LangChain tool design guide

rule.rationale: Retries are inherent in ReAct/Reflexion. Non-idempotent tools double-charge, duplicate writes, or corrupt state on retry.

rule.statement: Every pattern declares a terminal condition that is either deterministic (test command exits 0, schema validates) or operator-supplied (success_check callback). Vibes-based "the agent decides it's done" is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Reflexion without an objective check turns into max_attempts on every run. ReAct without one runs until the model "decides", which is a context overflow.

rule.statement: Reflexion's critic step uses a cheaper-or-equal model than the actor. Plan-and-Execute's planner may use a stronger model than the per-step executor.

rationale.source: OpenAI cookbook; production cost analysis at Anthropic / OpenAI

rule.rationale: Asymmetric model choice cuts cost 40-70% vs. running the same model in every role. Critics rarely need top-tier reasoning.

rule.statement: Every iteration emits a structured log line: {iteration, pattern, tool_name, tool_args_hash, result_summary, latency_ms, tokens}. No silent steps.

rationale.source: LangSmith / W&B Weave / Helicone observability conventions 2026

rule.rationale: Debugging an agent loop without per-iteration logs is impossible; failures look identical to successes. Logging is part of the contract, not optional polish.

rule.statement: For irreversible actions (file writes outside scratch dirs, API mutations, payments, sends), the pattern inserts a human-confirm gate BEFORE the call. Reflexion loops do not retry past a denied confirmation.

rationale.source: agent safety best practices; Anthropic responsible deployment guide

rule.rationale: Autonomous loops applied to destructive actions compound errors. The gate is the only reliable backstop when the success_check is wrong.

example.title: ReAct loop with all six rules
example.code: react = ReActAgent( tools=tools, model="claude-sonnet-4", max_iterations=10, # r1 log_emitter=structured_logger, # r5 ) # tools are idempotent (r2): each carries a dedup_key derived from args # terminal_condition = response without tool_calls (r3) # destructive tools wrap in human_confirm() (r6) react.run(task)
example.note: All six rules satisfied. Cost is predictable.

example.title: Unbounded Reflexion with side-effectful tools
example.note: No max_attempts (r1), no success_check (r3), destructive tools without confirm gate (r6). Will retry the same broken write until quota explodes.
